atoms.semantic_claim: The core `reth-evm` crate directly depends on `revm-context` and `revm-precompile` so async-aware execution context and precompile dispatch are available from the EVM layer itself.

destinations.evidence: The port moved explicit async EVM context enablement out of `reth-evm` and into the Ritual payload builder crate that owns async block-building.

In the ported dependency graph, enabling `revm/async-context` transitively enables `revm-precompile/async-context`, so the precompile half is now reached through dependency features rather than a direct `reth-evm` manifest entry.
The port actively initializes async context during Ritual payload execution, but that wiring lives in payload code rather than core `reth-evm`.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The async context and async precompile machinery still exists in the ported stack, but ownership moved from the core `reth-evm` crate to Ritual payload-building code plus revm feature propagation. This preserves the active Ritual payload path, but not the old `reth-evm`-level API surface.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Build a minimal consumer that depends only on `reth-evm` and `reth-evm-ethereum` and tries to initialize async precompile execution from the EVM layer. Downstream should compile and expose the async path; the port should require the Ritual payload crate or fail to provide equivalent core wiring.
atoms.human_question: Confirm that moving async context and precompile ownership out of core `reth-evm` and into Ritual payload/verifier crates is an acceptable API-surface change for downstream consumers.
atoms.notes: Behavior moved, but scope narrowed from core EVM crate to payload-builder-owned async execution.

atoms.semantic_claim: The downstream `reth-evm` crate owns async runtime helpers and observability for Ritual execution, including txpool access, TEE registry reads, metrics, and runtime support.

destinations.snippet:
reth-transaction-pool.workspace = true
ritual-async-inspector.workspace = true
ritual-async-fees.workspace = true
ritual-async-registry.workspace = true
ritual-tee-registry-reader.workspace = true
tracing.workspace = true
destinations.evidence: The ported payload-builder crate now carries the txpool, registry, and tracing dependencies that downstream had attached to `reth-evm`.

destinations.snippet:
use ritual_tee_registry_reader::{get_tee_registry_address, read_executor, Capability, ExecutorInfo};
use tracing::debug;
destinations.evidence: TEE-registry-backed verification was split into a dedicated verifier crate rather than staying in `reth-evm`.

destinations.snippet:
ritual-metrics.workspace = true
ritual-tee-registry-reader.workspace = true
reth-transaction-pool.workspace = true
destinations.evidence: The remaining Ritual node-level metrics and integration dependencies were rehomed into the nodebuilder side of the stack.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The added runtime/helper dependencies were not preserved in `reth-evm`; they were decomposed into port-side payload, verification, and node crates. The active Ritual execution pipeline still consumes txpool, registry, tracing, and metrics, but only through those split components.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Exercise the old `reth-evm`-owned async verification/pool-management path by importing `reth_evm::pool_manager` or running a short async settlement flow through `reth-evm` alone. Downstream supports that ownership model; the port should fail because the logic was redistributed across payload/verifier/node crates.
atoms.human_question: Confirm that rehoming txpool, registry, metrics, and async runtime support out of core `reth-evm` preserves the downstream Ritual execution path sufficiently for all consumers that previously depended on `reth-evm` directly.
atoms.notes: This is a real ownership move, not noise; downstream source code used these deps directly in `crates/evm/src/pool_manager.rs` and async tests.

atoms.semantic_claim: Building `reth-evm` with default features enables the async-context execution path by default.

destinations.evidence: The ported `reth-evm` crate does not enable async behavior by default.
A lower-layer dependency defaults async-context on, but `reth-evm` itself no longer exposes that default surface.
The port moved the default async entrypoint to the Ritual node crate instead of the reusable `reth-evm` crate.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Create a fresh crate that depends on `reth-evm` with default features and expects async-enabled APIs/behavior without extra feature flags. The downstream default build should satisfy that assumption; the ported `reth-evm` default build should not.
atoms.required_change: Restore or intentionally reject the downstream `reth-evm` default async-context contract so consumers know whether async execution is enabled by default.
atoms.notes: Related async defaults exist elsewhere in the dependency graph, but the specific `reth-evm` default contract is gone.

atoms.semantic_claim: The `reth-evm` crate exposes an `async-context` feature that forwards all required async execution feature gates through alloy, revm, ethereum primitives, and execution errors.

destinations.evidence: The ported `reth-evm` manifest has no `async-context` feature at all.
One downstream forwarding leg still exists in a dependency, but it is no longer surfaced by `reth-evm`.
The revm precompile feature still exists, but `reth-evm` does not forward to it.
The ported `reth-execution-errors` crate no longer provides the `async-context` forwarding target referenced by downstream.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Attempt to build a consumer with `reth-evm = { features = ["async-context"] }` and run an async-precompile execution path. Downstream should resolve the feature graph; the port should fail at manifest resolution or omit required async behavior because `reth-evm` no longer forwards the feature.
atoms.required_change: Restore or intentionally replace the `reth-evm` async-context feature surface and its forwarding chain through alloy, revm precompile, ethereum primitives, and execution errors.
atoms.notes: This is the clearest actionable gap in the ported `reth-evm` surface.

summary: The downstream `reth-evm` async-context manifest surface was split across port-side payload/verifier/node crates, but the `reth-evm` crate itself no longer exposes the same default or feature-gated async contract.
notes: Destination crate path is `crates/evm/evm/Cargo.toml` after the v2.2.0 split. Two atoms are moved ownership changes and two are direct missing feature-surface regressions for `reth-evm` consumers.
